title: 10 best herbal capsules for ankylosing spondylitis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for ankylosing spondylitis are a natural supplement designed to alleviate the symptoms of this chronic autoimmune disease. These capsules contain a blend of herbal extracts that help reduce inflammation, improve joint mobility, and relieve pain in the spine and other affected areas. Examples of herbal capsules that have shown promise include turmeric, ginger, and willow bark, which have anti-inflammatory properties. By taking these capsules regularly, patients with ankylosing spondylitis can experience improved flexibility, reduced stiffness, and enhanced overall well-being, allowing them to engage in daily activities with greater ease and confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation in the joints, which is a hallmark of ankylosing spondylitis. By reducing inflammation, curcumin helps to alleviate symptoms such as pain and stiffness, allowing for improved mobility and reduced disability. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, further contributing to its therapeutic benefits in managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory activity: Turmeric contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in AS by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and blocking the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Curcumin's antioxidant properties: Curcumin has potent antioxidant properties that help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue destruction in AS.

Turmeric's immunomodulatory effects: Turmeric contains other bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and preventing excessive joint damage in AS.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes contain high concentrations of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant also contain curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility in individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried turmeric roots of curcuma longa for sufficient quantity of powder.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 500mg of the turmeric powder and mix it with 250mg of glycerin and 50mg of water.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to maintain freshness.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called gingerol, which has been shown to reduce joint pain and inflammation. Additionally, ginger has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are chemicals that promote inflammation in the body. By reducing inflammation and pain, ginger capsules can help alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis, such as stiffness, swelling, and limited mobility, allowing individuals to manage their condition more effectively.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can reduce inflammation in the body, which is a key symptom of AS.
Pain relief: Ginger has been shown to have analgesic and antipyretic properties, making it effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with AS.

Gastrointestinal protection: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects can help protect the digestive tract from damage caused by inflammation and medication side effects common in AS patients.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the ginger plant, which contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Roots: The thickened roots of the ginger plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe joint pain and stiffness caused by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 100g of dried ginger root powder and sift through a fine-mesh strainer into a clean bowl.
Mix the ginger powder with 200ml of boiling water for exactly 10 minutes to create a potent decoction.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container discarding the solids.
Combine 30g of glycerin and 15g of distilled water in a small saucepan heating it gently over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the cooled ginger extract mixture and store them in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain harpagosides, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds help to reduce joint pain and stiffness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body. Additionally, devil's claw has been shown to improve joint mobility and flexibility, making it easier for individuals with ankylosing spondylitis to maintain a normal range of motion and perform daily activities without discomfort. By addressing these symptoms, devil's claw capsules can help alleviate the discomfort associated with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds: Devil's Claw contains anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds like harpagosides, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with AS.

Antioxidant properties: Devil's Claw's antioxidant properties help protect the joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the progression of joint degeneration in AS patients.
Muscle relaxant effects: The muscle relaxant compounds present in Devil's Claw capsules can help relieve stiffness and improve flexibility in the spine and joints, which is commonly affected in AS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the devil's claw plant contain harpagosides, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective in treating joint pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the devil's claw plant are used to extract its active compounds, including harpagoside, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried harpagophytum procumbens roots weighing approximately 250 grams.
Clean the harvested roots by washing them thoroughly with filtered water and pat drying.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 50 milliliters of organic vegetable oil with 20 grams of the powdered root mixture and let it steep in a dark glass jar for 2 weeks, shaking daily.

Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discard the solids and fill capsules with the resulting herbal oil at a dosage of 500 milligrams per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin. This ingredient effectively targets the inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis, which is a chronic inflammatory condition that affects the spine and pelvis. By reducing inflammation, white willow capsules can help alleviate symptoms such as stiffness, pain, and decreased mobility, allowing individuals with ankylosing spondylitis to experience improved joint flexibility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, a group of hormones that cause pain and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in white willow, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a key feature of ankylosing spondylitis and can alleviate symptoms like joint stiffness and pain.

Gut-soothing properties: White willow has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, reducing the risk of stomach ulcers and gastrointestinal bleeding that may occur when taking nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) or other medications commonly used to treat ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the white willow plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Barks: The barks of the white willow plant have been used for centuries to treat various inflammatory conditions, including arthritis and rheumatism, making it effective for managing symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried willow bark of salix alba, ensuring its quality and purity for optimal medicinal properties.
Grind the harvested willow bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for efficient extraction.
Mix 500mg of the powdered salix alba with 1000mg of carrier herb such as cornstarch or silica in a small bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal tightly to prevent contamination or moisture absorption.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container, protecting them from light, heat, and moisture for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the spine and joints, alleviating symptoms such as pain and stiffness. The capsules also increase collagen production, which can help improve joint mobility and flexibility. Additionally, stinging nettle has been shown to suppress inflammatory cytokines, further reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. By addressing these underlying causes of ankylosing spondylitis, stinging nettle capsules provide a natural approach to managing the condition and improving overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Stinging nettle contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis such as joint pain and stiffness.

Antioxidant properties: Stinging nettle is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, a common complication of chronic inflammatory conditions like ankylosing spondylitis.

Immune modulating effects: Stinging nettle has been shown to have immunomodulatory properties, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that contribute to the development and progression of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Stinging nettle roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce joint inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Rhyzomes: Nettle rhyzomes have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for relieving the chronic pain and stiffness characteristic of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves and stems from a trusted source, ensuring adequate quantity for processing.
Dry the harvested nettle material in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator, taking 24 to 48 hours.
Grind dried nettle into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, aim for 1 gram of nettle per capsule.
Mix 250 milligrams of nettle powder with 50 milligrams of other herbs of your choice, such as ginger or turmeric, in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal blend, encapsulating 500 milligrams per dose for daily intake.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the joints and spine. This natural remedy can help alleviate symptoms such as stiffness, pain, and limited mobility associated with this chronic condition. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to joint damage and inflammation. By taking frankincense capsules regularly, individuals with ankylosing spondylitis may experience relief from their symptoms and improved overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce joint swelling and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness.

Antioxidant: Frankincense's antioxidant properties can help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which is common in autoimmune conditions like ankylosing spondylitis.

Immune-modulating: Frankincense has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and joint damage by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and increasing the production of anti-inflammatory ones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Frankincense roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective in reducing joint inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: Frankincense barks contain a high concentration of boswellic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduce inflammation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality boswellia serrata roots and dry them in a warm place for at least two days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered root with 100 milligrams of gelatin and 50 milligrams of magnesium stearate.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.
Take two capsules three times daily after meals with water for four to six weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain adaptogenic properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as stiffness, pain, and fatigue. The herb's ability to modulate the immune response also helps decrease cytokine production, which can contribute to joint damage. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like withanolides, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
Rhyzomes: Contains a higher concentration of alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids that contribute to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ashwagandha roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated weighing out 250 grams per batch.
Grind the dried ashwagandha roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered ashwagandha with 500 milliliters of boiling water allowing it to steep for 45 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter discarding the solids and adding honey to taste.
Fill empty capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the strained liquid mixture and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it contains a natural compound that effectively reduces inflammation in the joints. This anti-inflammatory property helps to alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis, allowing patients to enjoy improved mobility and flexibility. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yanhusuo capsules can reduce inflammation in the joints, which helps alleviate pain and stiffness associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis.
Pain-relieving property: The capsules contain analgesic compounds that help to relieve pain and discomfort caused by joint inflammation and swelling.
Immune-modulating property: Yanhusuo capsules can regulate the immune system's response, which helps to reduce the excessive inflammation characteristic of Ankylosing Spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of starch and mucilage, making them effective in reducing inflammation and improving joint mobility in patients with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Dry 30g of dried corydalis yanhusuo roots in shade for two weeks ensuring complete moisture removal.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 15g of the powdered corydalis yanhusuo with 10g of refined white sugar and 5g of cornstarch in a bowl.
Add 20ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir until it forms a uniform paste preparing for encapsulation.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g each of the herbal mixture and seal immediately storing them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Longleaf pine capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain a unique blend of natural compounds that work to reduce inflammation and improve flexibility in the spine. The anti-inflammatory properties of longleaf pine, such as caryophyllene and terpenes, help to alleviate the chronic pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis, allowing for greater range of motion and reduced discomfort. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Longleaf Pine contains polyphenolic compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation in the joints and spine associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis.

Antioxidant activity: The capsules contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, protecting against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by chronic inflammation, which may contribute to the progression of Ankylosing Spondylitis.

Pain-relieving terpenoids: Longleaf Pine contains terpenoid compounds like limonene and alpha-pinene, which have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pinus palustris needles in the early morning when they are most aromatic and potent, collecting at least one ounce for processing.
Dry the collected needles using shade or low-temperature drying methods within 24 hours of harvesting, ensuring even moisture distribution throughout the material.
Crush the dried pinus palustris needles into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle, taking care to minimize heat generation during the process.

Mix the powdered herb with 10% gelatin or kosher salt at a ratio of one part herb to two parts binder, and fill empty capsules with the mixture, using a capsule machine according to manufacturer's instructions.

Store the filled capsules in an airtight container, protecting them from light, moisture, and heat, allowing the herbal extract to mature for at least four weeks before consumption, following label directions for recommended dosage and preparation times.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mojave yucca capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that work together to reduce inflammation and joint stiffness. The plant's active ingredients, such as saponins and polyphenols, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, thereby alleviating symptoms like pain and morning stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis. remedy_recipe: Gather fresh yucca schidigera roots dried in shade and grind into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 2000 milligrams of ground yucca schidigera powder with 1000 milligrams of magnesium stearate and 500 milligrams of silica dioxide.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixed herbal blend and seal immediately to prevent contamination.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container protected from light and heat for up to six months or one year in refrigeration.
Take three capsules twice daily before meals or as directed by a healthcare professional for optimal relief from ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint pain and stiffness. Additionally, maidenhair tree has been traditionally used to treat conditions related to inflammation, making it a potential natural remedy for ankylosing spondylitis patients seeking alternative or complementary treatment options.

remedy_recipe: Grind 250mg of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 100mg of dried turmeric root powder in a small bowl.
Add 50ml of cold-pressed virgin coconut oil to the mixture and stir until a smooth paste forms within 3 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the ginkgo-turmeric paste using an encapsulation machine for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain a blend of natural herbs that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the spine. The anti-inflammatory properties of elder capsules help to slow down or stop the progression of the disease, which can lead to permanent fusion of vertebrae. Barks: Elder bark contains antioxidants and phenolic compounds that help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage caused by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers at dawn in summer ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the collected flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours and store in airtight containers.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using an electric coffee grinder within 24 hours of drying.
Mix 2500mg of powdered sambucus nigra with 1000mg of silica gel as a filler and capsule mold material.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and seal immediately storing in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties. The extract of licorice root, specifically glycyrrhizin, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the joints and spine, thus alleviating symptoms such as stiffness, pain, and swelling. remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Reduces inflammation and prevents joint damage by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are elevated in AS patients.

Flavonoids: Exhibits anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, protecting joints from oxidative stress and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of AS such as pain and stiffness.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties which can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

remedy_recipe: Dry 100g of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered root with 250ml of boiling distilled water to create a decoction and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Filter the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove any solids and retain the liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a potent anti-inflammatory that reduces inflammation and pain in the joints. Boroleic acid: Boroleic acid, a compound found in borage, has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Lupane triterpenoids: Lupane triterpenoids, a type of saponin found in borage, have been demonstrated to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help mitigate the progression of joint damage and inflammation characteristic of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borage flowers in the morning when dew is still present on leaves and flowers.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 6-8 hours.
Grind dried flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part ground borage with 3 parts carrier material like rice flour or potato starch in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Turmeric's curcumin: Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and joint pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Ginger's gingerols and shogaols: These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate pain and stiffness in the joints and spine.

Boswellia's boswellic acids: Boswellic acids have been shown to reduce inflammation and joint damage by inhibiting enzymes involved in inflammation, which could potentially slow down disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and other nutrients, they help reduce inflammation and promote joint health.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried capsicum annuum powder and gelatin capsules of size zero.
Mix one teaspoon of dried capsicum annuum powder with one tablespoon of coconut oil for five minutes.
Add 100 milliliters of warm water to the mixture and stir until it forms a smooth paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with one-half teaspoon of the capsicum annuum paste and seal tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ankylosing spondylitis with herbal capsules is to use a combination of Turmeric (Curcuma longa) and Boswellia (Boswellia serrata). Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce joint pain and swelling, while Boswellia's ability to inhibit 5-LO enzyme activity alleviates inflammation. Additionally, Ginger (Zingiber officinale) can be used to enhance digestion and reduce nausea often associated with the condition. A consistent dosing schedule of these herbal capsules under medical supervision is essential for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ankylosing spondylitis that are treated with herbal capsules are osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and psoriatic arthritis. These conditions, characterized by joint pain and inflammation, can be managed with herbal supplements such as Turmeric/Curcumin, Ginger, Boswellia, Willow Bark, and Ashwagandha. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and stiffness, promoting joint health and well-being.
